description: Subtle multi-fault claim. The billing code 99214 is syntactically valid (Office Visit, Moderate Complexity) but the reported_procedure and report_category say this is an imaging study. Three compounding faults: (1) code is technically valid but categorically wrong for the reported service, (2) claimed amount $1,850 far exceeds the fee-schedule maximum for 99214 (~$150 office visit), (3) PLAN-BLUE covers 99214 for office visits but the service rendered was imaging — creating a category-coverage mismatch. Agent must validate (code is valid but wrong category), verify insurance, flag the discrepancy (category+amount mismatch), then reject.

context.reported_procedure: MRI Brain with IV Contrast — performed at Northside Neuroimaging Center (in-network). Patient: Robert T., 61M, MRN 00614872. Study date: 2024-03-25. Ordered by Dr. L. Chen, Neurology, for new-onset focal seizure evaluation. Gadolinium contrast administered (no adverse reaction). Findings: No acute intracranial pathology. Report signed by Dr. E. Mwangi.
context.clinical_notes: Claim submitted by billing department on 2024-03-28. Procedure performed: MRI Brain (CPT 70553 fee schedule: ~$620-$850). Submitted code 99214 is an office-visit code (fee schedule: ~$90-$150) — significant code-to-service mismatch. Claimed amount $1,850 exceeds fee-schedule ceiling for both 99214 (~$150) and 70553 (~$850). PLAN-BLUE covers 99214 for legitimate office visits but does NOT cover MRI brain (CPT 70553). This claim contains both a procedure category discrepancy and an amount discrepancy.
